objectives.title: Strategic Sovereignty
objectives.description: Anchoring within the EU legal, financial, and industrial ecosystem.

objectives.title: Legal & Jurisdictional Sovereignty
objectives.description: Legal environment, foreign authority exposure, and enforceability of rights.

objectives.title: Data & AI Sovereignty
objectives.description: Protection, control, and independence of data and AI services within the EU.

objectives.title: Operational Sovereignty
objectives.description: Ability to run, support, and evolve technology independently of foreign control.

objectives.title: Supply Chain Sovereignty
objectives.description: Geographic origin, transparency, and resilience of the technology supply chain.

objectives.title: Technology Sovereignty
objectives.description: Openness, transparency, and independence in the underlying technology stack.

objectives.title: Security & Compliance Sovereignty
objectives.description: Security operations and compliance controlled within the EU.

objectives.title: Environmental Sustainability
objectives.description: Long-term autonomy relating to energy usage and resource dependency.

sealLevels.label: SEAL-0
sealLevels.name: No Sovereignty
sealLevels.description: Service or operations under exclusive non-EU control, governed entirely outside the EU.

sealLevels.label: SEAL-1
sealLevels.name: Jurisdictional Sovereignty
sealLevels.description: EU law formally applies with limited enforceability; exclusive non-EU control remains.

sealLevels.label: SEAL-2
sealLevels.name: Data Sovereignty
sealLevels.description: EU law is enforceable but material non-EU dependencies and indirect non-EU control remain.

sealLevels.label: SEAL-3
sealLevels.name: Digital Resilience
sealLevels.description: EU actors have meaningful influence; only marginal non-EU control remains.

sealLevels.label: SEAL-4
sealLevels.name: Full Digital Sovereignty
sealLevels.description: Complete EU control subject only to EU law, with no critical non-EU dependencies.

questions.text: Where are the bodies with decisive authority over your cloud services located?
What assurances exist against change of control that could shift governance outside the EU?
To what extent does the provider rely on EU financing and create jobs and value within the EU?
Can operations continue securely if the vendor is pressured to cease service or withdraw support?
Which national legal system governs the provider's operations and customer contracts?
What is your exposure to non-EU laws with extraterritorial reach (e.g. US CLOUD Act, Chinese Cybersecurity Law)?
Could non-EU authorities compel access to data or systems through legal, contractual, or technical channels?
Where is intellectual property for the service created, registered, and developed?
Who holds effective control over cryptographic access to customer data?
How visible and auditable is data access, including use of AI models on your data?
Are storage and processing strictly confined to European jurisdictions without fallback to third countries?
Are AI models and data pipelines developed, trained, hosted, and governed under EU control?
How easily can workloads migrate or integrate with alternative EU-controlled solutions?
Can EU operators manage and support the service without requiring non-EU vendor involvement?
Is there an EU-based talent pool and is operational support delivered from within the EU under EU law only?
Is full technical documentation available, and are critical subcontractors under EU legal control?
What is the geographic source of key hardware and where is it manufactured or assembled?
What is the jurisdiction and provenance of firmware and embedded code controlling hardware?
Where is software architected, packaged, distributed, and updated—and under which jurisdiction?
How much reliance is there on non-EU vendors, and is the full supplier chain visible with audit rights?
Can the solution integrate via well-documented, non-proprietary APIs and publicly governed standards?
Is core software available under open licenses with rights to audit, modify, and redistribute?
How transparent is the architecture, including data flows and dependencies?
What degree of EU independence exists in processors, accelerators, and related software ecosystems?
What EU and internationally recognised security certifications has the provider attained?
Do security operations centres and monitoring operate exclusively under EU jurisdiction with customer oversight?
Can security patches be developed and applied independently of non-EU vendors?
Can EU entities perform independent security and compliance audits with full access?
Does infrastructure use energy-efficient design (e.g. low PUE) with measurable improvement targets?
Are circular economy practices applied to hardware reuse, refurbishment, and end-of-life?
Are carbon emissions, water usage, and other sustainability indicators measured and disclosed?
Is renewable or low-carbon energy used to power infrastructure and operations?
